invocation_rule_proof: Each pipeline stage maps to exactly one agent identity with a fixed model pin. Stage 7 validator uses a PERSISTED STRICT-ALTERNATION counter (.conductor/validator-alternation.json): read last_used, dispatch the opposite validator, flip last_used. This is a hard 50/50 guarantee across consecutive runs, not a statistical tendency (SHA-256 parity was rejected as non-exact).
indivisible_count_exception: Plan reviewer (Stage 2) ineligible: creator (Stage 1) is OpenAI SOL same family. Persisted strict alternation guarantees exactly one Tera and one M3 validation per two consecutive runs.
ongoing_usage_calculation: Plan reviewer (Stage 2): always M3. Test runner (Stage 6): always Tera. Validator (Stage 7): strictly alternating Tera/M3 - one of each per two consecutive runs (hard guarantee). Over a two-run cycle: 3 M3 + 3 Tera = exactly 50% each.

deterministic_alternation_rule.algorithm: strict-alternation (persisted counter); last_used field flips each Stage 7 dispatch
deterministic_alternation_rule.guarantee: exactly 50% Tera / 50% M3 per two consecutive validation runs
deterministic_alternation_rule.rejected_design: SHA-256(track_id) last-hex parity - rejected 2026-07-16 because it cannot guarantee an exact split on consecutive tracks
